channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Accused Killer of Charlie Kirk Expresses Regret in Court Testimony
item.description: The case against Tyler Robinson, accused of murdering influential right-wing campaigner Charlie Kirk, has entered its fourth day of hearings to determine if there is sufficient evidence for trial. Rob...

item.title: Tactical Battles to Watch in France vs Morocco World Cup 2026 Quarter-Final
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final between France and Morocco promises to be a highly anticipated match, with both teams showcasing strong form. France, led by Kylian Mbappé, boasts attacking firep...

item.title: Sarah Dugdale and Richard Harmon's Relationship: A Closer Look
item.description: The article discusses Sarah Dugdale, the star of 'Virgin River,' and her current relationship with fellow Canadian actor Richard Harmon. The couple made their relationship public on Instagram in April...

item.title: Supreme Court Dismisses Petitions Against 2022 Traffic Code
item.description: The Supreme Court of the Philippines has dismissed petitions challenging the controversial 2022 traffic code, citing procedural defects and the enactment of a new regulatory framework. The court's dec...

item.title: Ted Cruz Praises Democratic Candidate in Texas Senate Race Against Ken Paxton
item.description: Sen. Ted Cruz (R-TX) has publicly endorsed Democratic candidate James Talarico in the race against Republican Attorney General Ken Paxton, who was backed by former President Donald Trump. During a cal...

item.title: Daniel Bwala Takes Legal Action Against Al Jazeera Over Interview Controversy
item.description: Daniel Bwala, a key political figure and Special Adviser to President Tinubu, has launched a legal battle against Al Jazeera for allegedly defaming him through selective editing of his interview with ...

item.title: Data Breach Exposes Sensitive Information from Madison Square Garden's Database
item.description: A security breach at Madison Square Garden (MSG) exposed sensitive data, including tax documents of a junior employee, Salesforce records with millions of customer details, and internal databases cont...

item.title: South Canberra family targeted by hooded burglars in three separate incidents
item.description: A young family in South Canberra is in fear after experiencing three separate burglaries in a single night. The incident, which occurred on June 28, involved hooded men who broke into their cars and g...

item.title: Calgary Mayor Proposes Year-Round 24/7 Transit Service Inspired by Stampede Success
item.description: Calgary Mayor Jeromy Farkas has proposed extending the 24-hour transit service currently used during the Calgary Stampede to operate year-round. The idea stems from the success of the Stampede's expan...

item.title: Beit Bridge border guards face critical resource shortages and outdated technology challenges
item.description: South Africa's busiest land border, Beit Bridge, is operating under severe resource constraints with only 40 body cameras available for approximately 600 border guards. The Portfolio Committee on Home...

item.title: Sidney Crosby's Relationship with Kathy Leutner and His Sexual Orientation Clarified
item.description: The article explores Sidney Crosby's personal life, clarifying that he is not gay and has been in a long-term relationship with model Kathy Leutner since 2008. Crosby, a Canadian NHL superstar, is kno...

item.title: Efforts to Save Philippine Mangroves Amid Climate Threats
item.description: The article highlights the critical role of mangroves in protecting the Philippines from typhoons and coastal erosion. With over 60% of mangrove cover lost since 1918, environmentalists like Andrea Pi...

item.title: State Police Reform to Empower Governors as Chief Security Officers
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State emphasized that establishing state police forces will empower governors to act as genuine chief security officers, addressing persistent insecurity in the country...

item.title: First local bird flu case confirmed in South Australia, wildlife at risk
item.description: Australian authorities have confirmed the first case of H5N1 bird flu in a local bird species, raising concerns about the virus's spread to wildlife. A juvenile New Zealand fur seal in New South Wales...

item.title: Manila road closures announced for July 10 anti-corruption rally
item.description: The Manila Police District has announced temporary road closures around Liwasang Bonifacio, Mendiola, and Ayala Bridge on July 10 for an 'anti-corruption' rally led by the United People's Initiative (...

item.title: USA to Share 2026 World Cup Prize Money with Women's Team
item.description: The USA men's football team will share their 2026 FIFA World Cup prize money with the women's national team, following a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) signed in 2022. The deal, which came afte...

item.title: Over 1,000 Nigerians Evacuated from South Africa as Fifth Batch Arrives in Lagos
item.description: The Federal Government's evacuation of Nigerians from South Africa has reached a new milestone as the fifth batch of returnees arrived in Lagos on Thursday evening. The Air Peace flight, which brought...

item.title: National Treasury Withholds Funding from KZN Municipalities Over Governance Failures
item.description: The National Treasury has temporarily withheld equitable share transfers from seven KwaZulu-Natal municipalities due to governance failures, sparking concerns about the impact on vulnerable communitie...

item.title: Tanzania invests in traditional medicine research to bridge healthcare gap
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced plans to bring together experts from various fields to conduct comprehensive research on traditional and herbal medicines. This initiative aims to integrate indi...

item.title: Trump's alleged Iran strike aimed to dominate NATO summit attention, biographer claims
item.description: A Trump biographer suggests the president's recent strike on Iran was a deliberate move to dominate attention at the NATO summit, according to a podcast interview. Michael Wolff, author of 'Fire and F...
